verdict_reasoning: HCK is a Src family non-receptor tyrosine kinase with N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. This lipidation anchors it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane and intracellular membranes; it is not exposed on the outer face. Although HCK associates with activated surface receptors (e.g., Fc receptors) as a cytoplasmic signaling partner, the surface receptors themselves are the membrane anchors, not HCK. HCK lacks a transmembrane domain or GPI anchor and cannot be engaged by an extracellular binder from outside the cell.
